title: Data postprocessing
code: # Read read_classes output class_path <- "/path/to/read_classes.tsv" class_data <- ninetails::read_class_single(class_path)  # Read nonadenosine_residues output residue_path <- "/path/to/nonadenosine_residues.tsv" residue_data <- ninetails::read_residue_single(residue_path)

title: Data postprocessing
text: experiments multiple samples, use read_class_multiple() read_residue_multiple() metadata table: Note: Required metadata columns: sample_name, group, class_path, residue_path
code: # Define metadata table samples_table <- data.frame(   sample_name = c("sample_1", "sample_2", "sample_3", "sample_4"),   group = c("control", "control", "treatment", "treatment"),   class_path = c(     "/path/to/sample_1/read_classes.tsv",     "/path/to/sample_2/read_classes.tsv",     "/path/to/sample_3/read_classes.tsv",     "/path/to/sample_4/read_classes.tsv"   ),   residue_path = c(     "/path/to/sample_1/nonadenosine_residues.tsv",     "/path/to/sample_2/nonadenosine_residues.tsv",     "/path/to/sample_3/nonadenosine_residues.tsv",     "/path/to/sample_4/nonadenosine_residues.tsv"   ) )  # Read all files at once class_data <- ninetails::read_class_multiple(samples_table) residue_data <- ninetails::read_residue_multiple(samples_table)

title: Data postprocessing
text: Alternatively, provide metadata configuration file: Example config.yml:
code: config <- yaml::yaml.load_file("config.yml") samples_table <- data.frame(t(sapply(config$samples, unlist))) rownames(samples_table) <- NULL  class_data <- ninetails::read_class_multiple(samples_table) residue_data <- ninetails::read_residue_multiple(samples_table) samples:   sample_1:     sample_name: sample_1     group: control     class_path: /path/to/sample_1/read_classes.tsv     residue_path: /path/to/sample_1/nonadenosine_residues.tsv   sample_2:     sample_name: sample_2     group: treatment     class_path: /path/to/sample_2/read_classes.tsv     residue_path: /path/to/sample_2/nonadenosine_residues.tsv

title: Data postprocessing
text: Sometimes nucleotides -rich transcript ends misidentified poly() tails. Use reclassify_ninetails_data() minimize segmentation artifacts: Note: Apply function analysis.
code: ninetails_data <- ninetails::reclassify_ninetails_data(   residue_data = residue_data,   class_data = class_data,   grouping_factor = "sample_name",   transcript_column = "ensembl_transcript_id_short",   ref = "mmusculus" )  # Retrieve corrected data frames class_data <- ninetails_data[[1]] residue_data <- ninetails_data[[2]]

title: Data postprocessing
code: library(ninetails)  # 1. Load data from multiple samples samples <- data.frame(   sample_name = c("WT_1", "WT_2", "KO_1", "KO_2"),   group = c("WT", "WT", "KO", "KO"),   class_path = c("WT_1_classes.tsv", "WT_2_classes.tsv",                  "KO_1_classes.tsv", "KO_2_classes.tsv"),   residue_path = c("WT_1_residues.tsv", "WT_2_residues.tsv",                    "KO_1_residues.tsv", "KO_2_residues.tsv") )  class_data <- ninetails::read_class_multiple(samples) residue_data <- ninetails::read_residue_multiple(samples)  # 2. Reclassify to remove artifacts ninetails_data <- ninetails::reclassify_ninetails_data(   residue_data = residue_data,   class_data = class_data,   grouping_factor = "sample_name",   transcript_column = "ensembl_transcript_id_short",   ref = "mmusculus" )  class_data <- ninetails_data[[1]] residue_data <- ninetails_data[[2]]  # 3. Count classifications class_counts <- ninetails::count_class(class_data, grouping_factor = "group")  # 4. Merge and summarize merged <- ninetails::merge_nonA_tables(class_data, residue_data) summary <- ninetails::summarize_nonA(merged, summary_factors = "group")  # 5. Statistical comparison fisher <- ninetails::calculate_fisher(   class_data,   grouping_factor = "group",   comparison = c("WT", "KO") )

title: Visual inspection of signals
text: Draw entire signal (squiggle) given read: Parameters: readname: Read identifier nanopolish: Path Nanopolish polya output sequencing_summary: Path sequencing summary workspace: Path directory multi-fast5 files basecall_group: Fast5 hierarchy level (default: “Basecall_1D_000”) moves: TRUE, show move transitions background rescale: TRUE, scale signal picoamperes (pA) plot shows vertical lines marking poly() tail boundaries: Navy blue: 5’ end Red: 3’ end
code: plot <- ninetails::plot_squiggle_fast5(   readname = "0226b5df-f9e5-4774-bbee-7719676f2ceb",   nanopolish = system.file('extdata', 'test_data',                            'nanopolish_output.tsv',                            package = 'ninetails'),   sequencing_summary = system.file('extdata', 'test_data',                                    'sequencing_summary.txt',                                    package = 'ninetails'),   workspace = system.file('extdata', 'test_data',                           'basecalled_fast5',                           package = 'ninetails'),   basecall_group = 'Basecall_1D_000',   moves = FALSE,   rescale = TRUE ) print(plot)

title: Annotate ninetails output data with biomaRt — annotate_with_biomart
text: input_data Data frame. Tabular output ninetails pipeline. Must contain column named ensembl_transcript_id_short. attributes_to_get Character vector. Annotation attributes retrieve biomaRt. Default: c("ensembl_transcript_id", "external_gene_name", "description", "transcript_biotype"). filters Character string. Column input data frame match target mart. Default: "ensembl_transcript_id". organism Character string NULL. Organism shorthand built-mart presets. Currently available: "athaliana" Arabidopsis thaliana (Ensembl Plants) "hsapiens" Homo sapiens (Ensembl) "mmusculus" Mus musculus (Ensembl) "scerevisiae" Saccharomyces cerevisiae     (Ensembl Fungi) Mutually exclusive mart_to_use. Default: NULL. mart_to_use Mart object NULL. mart object created useMart useEnsembl. Mutually exclusive organism. Default: NULL.

code: calculate_fisher(   ninetails_data,   transcript_id_column = "ensembl_transcript_id_short",   min_reads = 0,   min_nonA_reads = 0,   grouping_factor = "sample_name",   condition1 = NA,   condition2 = NA,   alpha = 0.05,   base = "C",   ... )

title: Perform Fisher's exact test per transcript with BH p-value adjustment — calculate_fisher
text: ninetails_data Data frame. output merge_nonA_tables (merged tabular output containing read classification non-position data). transcript_id_column Character string. Column transcript ID data. Default: "ensembl_transcript_id_short"; can changed user. min_reads Numeric. Minimum number reads representing given transcript include analysis. Default: 0. Keep mind including many transcripts low coverage increases risk rejecting true null hypothesis (Benjamini-Hochberg procedure). min_nonA_reads Numeric. Minimum number reads containing non-adenosine residues (sum C, G, U) per transcript include analysis. prevents considering many observations non-significant p-value adjustment. Non--containing reads typically small fraction total pool, additional filtering can provide meaningful results. Default: 0. grouping_factor Character string. Name grouping variable. Default: "sample_name". condition1 Character string. First level grouping_factor use comparison. Required grouping_factor 2 levels. condition2 Character string. Second level grouping_factor use comparison. Required grouping_factor 2 levels. alpha Numeric. Significance threshold FDR. Default: 0.05. base Character string. Letter representing non-nucleotide statistics computed. Accepted values: "C", "G", "U", "" (non-residues combined). Default: "C". ... Additional parameters passed nonA_fisher (development).

text: Legacy mode. Due Oxford Nanopore Technologies' transition Guppy Dorado, Fast5 POD5, R9 R10 chemistry, pipeline maintained backward compatibility optimised. current data, use check_tails_dorado_DRS instead. function accepts either nanopolish tailfindr output (DRS ). Feature extraction     (create_tail_feature_list). Tail segmentation     (create_tail_chunk_list). GAF computation (create_gaf_list). CNN classification     (predict_gaf_classes). Output creation (create_outputs). comments column output encodes detailed read classification: IRL Insufficient read length. QCF Nanopolish QC failed. MAU Move transition absent, non-residue undetected. MPU Move transition present, non-residue undetected. NIN included analysis (pass_only = TRUE). YAY Move transition present, non-residue detected. Filtering criteria: basecaller move value = 1, qc_tag %% c("PASS") (c("PASS", "SUFFCLIP")), nanopolish-estimated tail length >= 10 nt.

Start coordinate poly() tail     (renamed tail_start). tail_end Integer. End coordinate poly() tail     (retained tailfindr). polya_length Numeric. title: Converts tailfindr results to format compatible with ninetails — convert_tailfindr_output

R9.4.1 pore     detection region spans 5 nucleotides, tails shorter 10 nt     (2 full adjacent 5-mers overlap) unreliable. Therefore     qc_tag set "PASS" tails >= 10 nt     "NOREGION" otherwise.

title: Create Ninetails output tables for Dorado DRS pipeline — create_outputs_dorado
text: dorado_summary_dir Character string. Path directory containing Dorado summary files (.txt, .tsv, .csv) per-read poly() tail information reads passed Ninetails quality control filtering. files contain reads meeting four QC criteria: () mapped reference, (II) MAPQ > 0, (III) valid poly() coordinates (poly_tail_start != 0), (IV) poly() tail length >= 10 nt. nonA_temp_dir Character string. Path directory containing non-adenosine prediction RDS files generated CNN models. RDS file contains predictions signal chunks, stored either named vectors lists 'chunkname' 'prediction' elements. polya_chunks_dir Character string. Path directory containing poly() chunk RDS files. files contain segmented signal chunks positional information (chunk_start_pos, chunk_end_pos) used calculating estimated positions non-residues along poly() tail. num_cores Integer. Number cores use parallelized file loading processing. Must positive integer. Default 1. Parallel processing uses foreach doSNOW backend displays progress bars file loading operations. qc Logical. Whether apply quality control filtering terminal predictions. TRUE (default), predictions within 2 nt either end poly() tail removed reduce false positives adapter-tail boundary artifacts. Reads terminal predictions reclassified blank (MPU). original_summary Character string data frame. Path original unfiltered Dorado summary file, data frame . file contain reads sequencing run, including failed QC filtering. Required complete read accounting - function classify reads, including filtered preprocessing. Must contain columns: filename, read_id, poly_tail_length, poly_tail_start, poly_tail_end, alignment_genome, alignment_direction, alignment_mapq.

title: Create Ninetails output tables for Dorado DRS pipeline — create_outputs_dorado

Includes columns: readname, contig,       polya_length, qc_tag (MAPQ), class (decorated/blank/unclassified),       comments (classification code). nonadenosine_residues Data frame per-chunk predictions       non-adenosine residues decorated reads . Includes columns:       readname, contig, prediction (C/G/U), est_nonA_pos (estimated position       3' end), polya_length, qc_tag (MAPQ). Empty dataframe       non-residues detected.

title: Create Ninetails output tables for Dorado DRS pipeline — create_outputs_dorado
text: function implements complete read accounting system classifies reads original summary file biologically meaningful categories based quality control metrics modification detection results. function intended used outside pipeline wrapper (standalone function).

title: Create Ninetails output tables for Dorado DRS pipeline — create_outputs_dorado
text: function classifies reads three main categories specific   biological interpretations: 1. decorated - Reads detected non-adenosine modifications Comment code: YAY (Yes, modification detected - Yeah!) Criteria: Read passed QC filters CNN detected least       one non-residue (C, G, U) survived terminal filtering Biological meaning: Poly() tail contains non-adenosine residues,       indicating potential guanylation, uridylation, cytidylation 2. blank - Reads without detected modifications MAU (Move transition Absent, Unmodified): signal deviations       detected; tail appears pure poly() MPU (Move transition Present, Unmodified): Signal deviations       detected CNN predicted residues, predictions       filtered terminal artifacts IRL (Insufficient Read Length): Poly() tail < 10 nt; short       reliable modification detection Biological meaning: Poly() tail contains adenosine residues       short/ambiguous modification calling 3. unclassified - Reads failed quality control UNM (UnMapped): Read unmapped reference (alignment_direction = "*"       alignment_mapq = 0); determine tail context BAC (BAd Coordinates): Invalid poly() tail coordinates       (poly_tail_start = 0); indicates segmentation failure Biological meaning: Technical failures preventing reliable       modification analysis; excluded biological interpretation

title: Create Ninetails output tables for Dorado DRS pipeline — create_outputs_dorado
text: Non-adenosine residue positions estimated using formula: est_nonA_pos = round(poly_tail_length - ((poly_tail_length * centr_signal_pos) / signal_length), 0) : centr_signal_pos: Mean chunk start end positions       signal space signal_length: 0.2 Ã— (poly_tail_end - poly_tail_start)       converts signal coordinates nucleotide space poly_tail_length: Total poly() tail length Dorado Positions reported distance 3' end tail   (position 1 = 3' nucleotide). Dorado pipeline, position   calculations rounded integers, unlike Guppy legacy pipeline   uses decimal precision.

title: Create Ninetails output tables for Dorado DRS pipeline — create_outputs_dorado
text: Reads dorado_summary_dir must meet four criteria   processed neural network: Mapped: alignment_direction "+" "-", "*" High mapping quality: alignment_mapq > 0 Valid coordinates: poly_tail_start != 0 (DRS, adapter       passes pore first, poly() start position 0) Sufficient length: poly_tail_length >= 10 nt Reads failing criterion marked appropriate comment codes   (UNM, BAC, IRL) still included output complete   read accounting.

title: Create Ninetails output tables for Dorado DRS pipeline — create_outputs_dorado
text: read_classes columns: readname Read identifier (UUID) contig Reference contig/transcript name polya_length Poly() tail length nucleotides qc_tag Mapping quality score (MAPQ) class Read classification: decorated, blank, unclassified comments 3-letter code: YAY, MAU, MPU, IRL, UNM, BAC nonadenosine_residues columns: readname Read identifier (UUID) contig Reference contig/transcript name prediction Predicted nucleotide: C, G, U (excluded) est_nonA_pos Estimated position 3' end (integer, 1-based) polya_length Total poly() tail length nucleotides qc_tag Mapping quality score (MAPQ)

title: Detect outliers (peaks and valleys) in ONT signal using z-scores — filter_signal_by_threshold
text: pseudomoves vector allows accurate calibration nucleotide positions potential non-adenosine residues moves produced Guppy basecaller. algorithm implements sliding-window approach based adaptive z-scores: calibration phase (100 synthetic data points drawn     frequent signal values) establishes baseline signal characteristics Rolling mean standard deviation computed 100-point     adaptive window Outliers detected signal deviates > 3.5 standard deviations     local mean Direction deviation determines pseudomove value Terminal positions (first 5) masked prevent false positives adapter-tail boundary. temporary safeguard resegmentation model refined.

title: Detection of outliers (peaks & valleys) in ONT signal using z-scores. — filter_signal_by_threshold_trainingset
text: algorithm prepends 100 calibration data points (sampled 10 frequent signal values first 10 observations) stabilise baseline estimate. applies sliding window 100 data points: observation exceeding 3.5 standard deviations running mean classified peak (+1) valley (-1); positions scored 0. trimming calibration prefix, terminal positions zeroed (first 5 last 5) prevent edge artefacts entering downstream chunk extraction. Finally, substitute_gaps applied fill isolated zero-gaps pseudomove vector.

title: Plot panel characteristics of ninetails output — plot_panel_characteristics
text: input_residue_data Data frame containing non-residue predictions. input_class_data Optional data frame containing read classification output ninetails. Mutually exclusive input_merged_nonA_tables_data. input_merged_nonA_tables_data Optional data frame returned merge_nonA_tables. Mutually exclusive input_class_data. type Character string. Either "default" "moderna". "moderna" option marks default UCUAG pentamer position (100 nt). max_length Numeric. Maximum poly() tail length displayed distribution panels. direction_5_prime Logical. TRUE (default), non-positions reported 5' end poly() tail. FALSE, positions recalculated relative 3' end.

title: Plot panel characteristics of ninetails output — plot_panel_characteristics
text: patchwork-assembled ggplot object containing panels –E.

title: Plot panel characteristics of ninetails output — plot_panel_characteristics
text: function assembles panels –E patchwork layout: – Read categories (blank, non-containing, total) B – Counts reads containing C, G, U residues C – Distribution poly() tail lengths D – Normalized distribution non-positions E – Raw distribution non-positions

title: Plot panel characteristics of ninetails output — plot_panel_characteristics
text: function internally subsets input data predefined set columns. additional columns present input data frames silently dropped. Therefore, columns listed must present supplied inputs.

title: Plot panel characteristics of ninetails output — plot_panel_characteristics
text: Must contain least following columns: readname Character. Unique read identifier. prediction Character factor. Predicted non-residue   (e.g., "C", "G", "U"). est_nonA_pos Numeric. Estimated position non-residue   within poly() tail. polya_length Numeric. Estimated poly() tail length. columns used : binning positions tail lengths (Panels D E), computing normalized residue frequencies, generating residue-level counts labels.

title: Plot panel characteristics of ninetails output — plot_panel_characteristics
text: Must contain least: readname Character. Required merging residue data. group Character factor. Experimental group identifier. input internally merged input_residue_data using merge_nonA_tables.

title: Plot panel characteristics of ninetails output — plot_panel_characteristics
text: Must output merge_nonA_tables contain least following columns (used tail distribution, summarization plotting): sample Character. Sample identifier. group Character factor. Experimental group identifier. readname Character. Unique read identifier. prediction Character factor. Non-residue prediction. est_nonA_pos Numeric. Estimated non-position. polya_length Numeric. Poly() tail length. class Character. Read classification label. comments Character. Additional classification comments. transcript Character. Transcript name. ensembl_transcript_id_full Character. Full Ensembl transcript ID. ensembl_transcript_id_short Character. Short Ensembl transcript ID. prediction_C Numeric logical. Indicator/count C residues. prediction_G Numeric logical. Indicator/count G residues. prediction_U Numeric logical. Indicator/count U residues. nonA_residues Character. Encoded non-residue information;   NA indicates blank tail. columns required internally retained : classification summaries (Panels B), tail length distributions (Panel C), grouping normalization logic, detection blank vs non-containing reads.

title: Plot panel characteristics of ninetails output — plot_panel_characteristics
text: input data must represent exactly one experimental group. multiple unique values detected group column, function stops error. Multiple samples within single group allowed.

text: nucleotide Character. One "", "C", "G", "U". Defines type signal filtering applied produce training data desired nucleotide context. nanopolish Character string. title: Filters out signals of a given nucleotide type for neural network training-set preparation. — prepare_trainingset
text: internal pipeline differs nucleotide: "" Uses -branch:     create_tail_feature_list_A \(\rightarrow\)     create_tail_chunk_list_A \(\rightarrow\)     create_gaf_list_A. "C" Uses non-branch value = -1     (valley filtering):     create_tail_feature_list_trainingset \(\rightarrow\)     create_tail_chunk_list_trainingset \(\rightarrow\)     filter_nonA_chunks_trainingset \(\rightarrow\)     create_gaf_list. "G" Uses non-branch value = 1     (peak filtering). "U" Uses non-branch value = -1     (valley filtering), filter direction C.

text: dorado_summary Character data frame. Path Dorado summary file data frame. pod5_dir Character. Directory containing pod5 files. num_cores Integer. Number CPU cores use. qc Logical. Whether perform quality control filtering. TRUE, applies following stringent filters ensure high-quality data: Alignment direction Removes unmapped reads (alignment_direction == "*") Mapping quality Removes reads zero mapping quality (alignment_mapq == 0) Poly() coordinates Removes reads invalid start positions (poly_tail_start == 0) Tail length Removes reads tails shorter 10 nucleotides (poly_tail_length < 10) filters remove low-quality reads, misaligned sequences, likely artifacts pore clogging basecalling errors. Set FALSE preliminary analysis using pre-filtered data. save_dir Character. Directory output files saved. prefix Character. Prefix add output file names (optional). part_size Integer. Number reads process chunk. cli_log Function logging messages progress.

title: Preprocess Dorado inputs for ninetails analysis (no BAM processing) — preprocess_inputs
text: quality control filtering (qc = TRUE) implemented via filter_dorado_summary performs following operations: Unmapped read removal: Reads alignment_direction = "*"     indicate failed alignment reference excluded. Low mapping quality removal: Reads alignment_mapq = 0     ambiguous poor-quality alignments excluded. Invalid coordinate removal: Reads poly_tail_start = 0     indicate failed poly() detection, often pore clogging artifacts. Short tail removal: Reads poly_tail_length < 10 nucleotides     lack sufficient poly() sequence reliable modification detection. criteria ensure high-confidence reads valid poly() tails proper genomic alignment processed, significantly improving downstream classification accuracy reducing false positive rates.
